hypothesis: UCB1 adherence rate >50% (sessions open in top-5 domain vs 15.2% random baseline)
method: Extracted all DOMEX lanes from SWARM-LANES.md for S380-S410 window (31 sessions). Mapped lane prefixes to domains using dispatch_optimizer.py's canonical PREFIX_TO_DOMAIN table. Retrieved current UCB1 scores from dispatch_optimizer.py --all --json. Top-5 UCB1 domains: nk-complexity(4.40), meta(4.32), expert-swarm(4.04), security(3.71), evaluation(3.61). Computed lane-level adherence (% of total DOMEX lanes in top-5 domains) and session-level adherence (% of DOMEX sessions that opened >=1 top-5 lane). Computed Spearman rho between UCB1 domain rank and actual S380-S410 dispatch frequency rank for the 21 common domains. Three domains (strategy, stochastic-processes, quality) had active DOMEX lanes in the window but were retired from dispatch optimizer (0 active frontiers) and excluded from rank correlation.
rank_correlation_note: Spearman rho=-0.146 (n=21 common domains, p~0.53). Near-zero negative correlation between current UCB1 rank and S380-S410 dispatch frequency rank. Meta (UCB1 #2) and nk-complexity (#1) are correctly top-ranked, but social-media (UCB1 #27) was dispatched 5 times (window rank #6), catastrophic-risks (UCB1 #6) had only 2 dispatches (window rank #17), and security (UCB1 #4) had only 2 dispatches (window rank #18). Correlation is weak because strategy (15 lanes, retired) and stochastic-processes (5 lanes, retired) are excluded from the rank comparison.
key_finding: Lane-level adherence is 45.0% — 3x above random baseline (15.2%) but below the 50% threshold, making the hypothesis PARTIAL (direction CONFIRMED, magnitude borderline). Session-level adherence is 90.5% (19/21 sessions had >=1 top-5 lane), strongly confirming that swarm consistently includes top-UCB1 domains per session. The dominant deviation pattern: strategy domain (15 lanes, rank #2 in window) was heavily dispatched despite having 0 active UCB1 frontiers (retired domain), crowding out adherent dispatches. When retired domains are excluded, adherence rises to 54.5%, crossing the 50% threshold. Rank correlation near zero (rho=-0.146) confirms UCB1 score rank does NOT predict full dispatch ordering — exploration-exploitation balance drives top-5 compliance but lower-ranked domains are dispatched opportunistically regardless of score.
lesson_recommendation: UCB1 top-5 adherence at lane level is 45% (3x random, PARTIAL), but session-level is 90.5% — nearly every session opens at least one UCB1-recommended domain. The swarm follows UCB1 at the session level but supplements with off-rank dispatches. Retired domains (strategy, stochastic-processes) account for 21/120 lanes (17.5%) and suppress adherence metric while not violating dispatch logic (no open frontiers = genuinely closed). Recommend tracking 'UCB1 available domains only' as the adherence denominator in future measurements.
